title: [ExportVerilog] Declare IR immutable during emission
body:
#2238 jumps through a few hoops to ensure that certain parts of Verilog emission only occur in sequential pieces of the code. However, it looks like the way ExportVerilog is written now, all mutation to the IR is limited to the preparation phase (`prepareHWModule`). Special handling of `sv.bind` (because it reaches into other modules) seems to be unnecessary now that preparation actually occurs in a distinct phase that is guaranteed to finish before the first emission calls occur (#2234).

How about we declare the IR as being immutable after preparation, such that emission code can freely poke at things without having to defer some ops to later sequential emission? I'm not sure how we would enforce something like this in the code though. In Rust you'd just hand out immutable references to everything, but I don't think there's a concept of an immutable operation handle or IR in MLIR, right @lattner?
